uplink.description: Battery, temperature, and humidity (TTN example bytes)
uplink._note: expected derived from upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js): battery 100%, temp 0x0118 LE = 280/10 = 28.0 C, humidity 109/2 = 54.5%. Input bytes are the upstream example; expected.data authored here (battery% -> batteryPercent extra; temp/hum -> air.*).

uplink.description: Negative temperature (synthetic, exercises signed LE decode)
uplink._note: expected derived from upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js): battery 80%, temp 0xFFF6 LE = -10/10 = -1.0 C, humidity 90/2 = 45%.
expected derived from upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js): battery 72%, temp 0x00DD LE = 221/10 = 22.1 C, humidity 84/2 = 42%.

uplink.description: Current reading plus one datalog history point (synthetic)
uplink._note: expected derived from upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js) for the 0x20/0xce datalog channel: current temp 250/10 = 25.0 C, hum 100/2 = 50%; history point timestamp 1699291264 (-> RFC3339 2023-11-06T17:21:04.000Z), temp 0x0128 LE = 296/10 = 29.6 C, humidity 90/2 = 45%. Upstream emits the prior reading in a flat history[] with a raw `timestamp`; we normalize each entry to vocabulary keys with an RFC3339 `time` per AUTHORING.md, keeping the current reading at the top level.

expected derived from upstream TTN decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js): an unrecognized channel id (0x09) is recognized by nothing, so upstream returns an empty object; our codec reports an error rather than emitting bare {}.
